entities.name: Didier Blondel
entities.description: EVP, Chief Financial Officer & Board Secretary of Abivax. Executive sponsor for finance and ERP decisions.
entities.notes: Finance executive lead; key stakeholder for board decision framing, budget readiness, and ERP governance.

entities.name: Hema Kesh
entities.description: Finance lead responsible for FP&A, treasury, tax, and accounting. Key stakeholder driving ERP scope.
entities.notes: No manufacturing in ERP; external CMOs. P2P + financial reporting critical. "DO NOT LET SUPPLY CHAIN DERAIL." French team "very prickly." Reports directly under Didier.

entities.name: Pat Maloy
entities.description: Investor relations lead handling fundraising and IR.
entities.notes: Finance-adjacent, not ERP core.

entities.name: Trinidad
entities.description: Accounting manager who drives the consolidation process.
entities.notes: French finance. Critical for consolidation.

entities.name: Frederick
entities.description: FP&A lead on the French finance team.
entities.notes: FP&A scope.

entities.name: Juliette
entities.description: P2P process owner on the French side. Reports to Philippe and has a staff AP person.

properties.reportsTo: Philippe Has staff AP person. Juliette

entities.notes: Critical Ã¢â‚¬â€ P2P process owner. Juliette + Kimberly = P2P team.

entities.name: Kimberly Gordon
entities.description: P2P contractor on the US side.
entities.notes: US contractor. Critical Ã¢â‚¬â€ P2P US side.

entities.name: Philippe
entities.description: Manager of Juliette in the P2P chain.
entities.notes: Juliette reports to Philippe.

entities.name: Matt Epley
entities.description: Boots-on-the-ground accountant handling SEC reporting, financial press releases, and equity tracking.
entities.notes: SEC/NASDAQ reporting, financial press, equity (Certent). ActivaÃ¢â‚¬â€US not thrilled; Hema/Matt main updaters. IFRS adjustments quarterly.

entities.name: Jade Nguyen
entities.description: IT team member based in France.

properties.role: IT lead/contact for ERP integration coordination

entities.notes: Pronounced Jahd. IT France-driven. Was in Paris conference room for NetSuite demo.

entities.name: Thelma
entities.description: Meeting organizer at Abivax.
entities.notes: Invited to NetSuite/Oracle demo Feb 12.

entities.name: Camille Girard
entities.description: KPMG contractor running the ERP RFP process. Main point of contact for vendor evaluation.
entities.notes: Main contact managing the RFP process. Meeting with Mike on Friday 2/14 to discuss SAP implementation costs and next steps. Was in Paris conference room for NetSuite demo.

entities.name: Venceslas d'Echallens
entities.description: NetSuite Customer Success Advisor. Part of NetSuite's delivery team for Abivax evaluation.
entities.notes: Led the NetSuite demo on 2/12. In Paris conference room. Part of the team handling all of Europe.

entities.name: Sage
entities.description: Current ERP system used by Abivax. Manual entry, lacks SOX controls. Must be replaced by Jan 1, 2027.
entities.notes: ~100 vendors, ~20 LLC invoices/month manually keyed. SOX controls not being met.

entities.name: SAP S/4HANA
entities.description: Proposed ERP replacement. Commercial offer presented with Finance, Supply Chain, Ariba, BTPEA modules.
entities.notes: No manufacturing in scope (external CMO model). Offer subject to SAP Finance approval.

entities.name: NetSuite
entities.description: Alternative ERP being evaluated. Demo completed Feb 12, 2026. In-house implementation by NetSuite Customer Success team.

scope: P2P (PO, vendor bills, 3-way matching, approval flows)

entities.notes: Demo completed 2/12. Total 5-year cost ~1.12M EUR (vs SAP 554k licenses + unknown implementation). OCR not native - red flag for P2P automation. Timeline can hit 1/1/27 with April kickoff. Single vendor accountability (NetSuite implements their own tool).

entities.name: Docushare
entities.description: Current document management used for P2P workflow. Described as a "glorified SharePoint."
entities.notes: Some drop-down functionality but no automation. Used in P2P workflow before Sage entry.

entities.name: Ariba
entities.description: SAP's procurement and invoice management solution. Part of SAP offer.
entities.notes: Camille has experience with Ariba. Compare vs Tungsten.

entities.name: Certent
entities.description: Equity tracking system owned by Insightful. Backend tool for equity accounting.
entities.notes: Not front-facing. Matt does all accounting and setup. IFRS adjustments booked quarterly.

entities.name: Activa
entities.description: French company system that manages equity/expense program for users.
entities.notes: US side not thrilledÃ¢â‚¬â€not user friendly. Hema/Matt main updaters. Operates like expense program accounting system.

entities.name: Adaptive
entities.description: FP&A planning tool currently in use.
entities.notes: Financial reporting currently done via exportsÃ¢â‚¬â€all manual.

entities.name: Abivax
entities.description: Biotech company headquartered in France with US operations. Pursuing ERP modernization ahead of US public launch.
entities.notes: Launching publicly in US end of 2027. All vendors are GxP compliant. External logistics providers for supply chain.

entities.name: Abivax France
entities.description: French entity of Abivax. Handles most IT and has finance team including consolidation.
entities.notes: French team "very prickly" per Hema. May be nervous about increased US hiring. Traditional center of the company.

entities.name: Abivax US
entities.description: US entity of Abivax. Growing as company prepares for US public launch.
entities.notes: ~20 LLC invoices per month, increasing. US public launch planned for end of 2027.

entities.name: PWC
entities.description: French auditor for Abivax.
entities.notes: French PWC is the auditor.

entities.name: KPMG
entities.description: Tax consultant used for 1099 process.
entities.notes: Currently consulted for 1099 process workaround.

entities.name: SAP (Company)
entities.description: Enterprise software vendor. Commercial offer pending approval.
entities.notes: Offer subject to SAP Finance approval; deadline end Q1 2026.

entities.name: Oracle
entities.description: Enterprise software vendor. Parent company of NetSuite.
entities.notes: NetSuite demo scheduled for Feb 12.

entities.name: Hema/Matt Intro Call
entities.description: Hema introducing Mike to Matt Epley. Overview of ERP priorities, P2P current state, org structure, and critical scope.
entities.notes: First substantive meeting. Established: P2P and Financial Reporting are critical scope, no manufacturing, go-live 1/1/27 non-negotiable, SOX material weaknesses need addressing.

entities.name: NetSuite/Oracle Demo
entities.description: NetSuite Customer Success team value proposition and scoping presentation. COMPLETED.

properties.location: Microsoft Teams (Salle Burrill B. Crohn - Paris conference room)
properties.attendees: Mike Markman (remote), Camille Girard, Jade Nguyen, Ben Alaya Aymen (Abivax Paris). Venceslas d'Echallens, Laurent Bailly, Levon, Jamal (NetSuite team)

entities.notes: Full scope presentation: R2R, P2P, Design to Build. Key numbers: 449k EUR implementation, 134k EUR/year licenses (670k over 5yr), total ~1.12M EUR. Timeline: April kickoff, Dec/Jan go-live (hits 1/1/27). OCR not included (ZoneCapture add-on required) - flagged as concern vs SAP Ariba native. French/US localization included. Multi-GAAP (IFRS, French GAAP, US GAAP) included. Mike asked about OCR - they confirmed it requires separate subscription.

entities.name: ERP Go-Live
entities.description: Target date for new ERP system to be live with P2P and Financial Reporting.
entities.notes: Non-negotiable. Need P2P and financial reporting live.

entities.name: US Public Launch
entities.description: Commercial launch in the US market.
entities.notes: ERP must support by go-live.

entities.name: SAP Offer Expiry
entities.description: Deadline for SAP commercial offer validity.
entities.notes: Conditions valid until end Q1 2026. Subject to SAP Finance approval.

entities.name: Sage Sunset
entities.description: Date when Sage will be decommissioned.
entities.notes: Define date; part of quarter leverage.

entities.name: Procure-to-Pay (P2P)
entities.description: End-to-end procurement process from purchase requisition through payment. Critical scope for ERP implementation.
entities.notes: Current process: Docushare for workflow, manual PO tracking, hand-keyed into Sage. ~100 vendors. SOX controls not being met. Essential to have PO approvals and budget monitoring.

entities.name: SOX Compliance
entities.description: Financial reporting and internal control requirements. Current P2P process has material weaknesses.
entities.notes: Material weaknesses haven't been addressed. Manual process is unsustainable. Not a material misstatement but audit issue. Investors aren't worried yet.

entities.name: IFRS
entities.description: International accounting standards used by Abivax (French parent). Requires adjustments for US GAAP.
entities.notes: French IFRS basis. Adjustments booked quarterly. Hema wants multi-GAAP capability in new ERP.

entities.name: US GAAP
entities.description: US accounting standards. Required for SEC reporting.
entities.notes: Need multi-GAAP support in new ERP for US/French compliance.

entities.name: Consolidation
entities.description: Process of combining financial statements from multiple entities (US + France).
entities.notes: Trinidad drives consolidation process. Critical need in ERP scope.

entities.name: Financial Reporting
entities.description: Journal entries, park/post, reporting by cost center. Critical ERP scope alongside P2P.
entities.notes: Currently manual exports. Want journal entries park/post, cost center reporting, consolidation.

entities.name: No Manufacturing Module
entities.description: Decision to exclude manufacturing from ERP scope. Abivax uses external CMOs.
entities.notes: No in-house manufacturing. All done by Sequence, Thermofisher, and others. Always should be external. "DO NOT LET SUPPLY CHAIN DERAIL THE IMPLEMENTATION."

entities.name: External CMO Model
entities.description: Business model where all manufacturing is outsourced to Contract Manufacturing Organizations.
entities.notes: All vendors are GxP compliant. When selling, 3rd party logistics provider handles fulfillment and provides reporting that feeds into sales. Customer-level details not needed in ERPÃ¢â‚¬â€comes from 3PL.

entities.name: Board Decision Communication
entities.description: Board meeting where ERP decision direction is communicated.
entities.notes: Decision communication point confirmed on IT welcome call.
